name: Insurance Agency
description: Quote-to-bind pipeline for a brokerage, with a Policy object holding the carrier, premium, effective date and renewal date so nothing lapses unnoticed.
ai_context: You are a CRM assistant for an insurance brokerage. Contacts are prospects, policyholders and named insureds; a deal is one quote-to-bind opportunity for a specific line of coverage, and the contract that results is tracked as a Policy. Never quote a premium, confirm that something is covered, or state what a policy pays or excludes unless that detail is already recorded on the record — coverage questions go to the carrier or a licensed producer. Do not invent carrier names, form numbers or underwriting decisions.

automation_rules.name: Book the needs analysis while the lead is warm
automation_rules.description: Insurance shoppers are quoting three brokers at once — this creates the call task the moment a contact lands.

params.title: New lead — information still needed
params.body: Capture before quoting: current carrier and expiring premium, X-date, loss runs or claims history, drivers or scheduled property, and any prior cancellation or non-renewal.

automation_rules.name: Keep the quote file current
automation_rules.description: Fires on every deal stage change so carrier submissions, quote comparisons and the Policy record stay in step with the pipeline.

params.title: Update carrier submissions and premium on {{deal.title}}

kb_templates.company:
## About the agency

[Agency name] is an independent brokerage in [city], licensed in [states / regions] since [year]. We place [personal lines / commercial lines / both].

## Appointments

We are appointed with [list your carriers]. For risks outside that list we broker through [wholesaler / MGA].

## Contact

Office address, phone, after-hours claims line, and licence numbers where you are required to display them.
kb_templates.products:
## Lines we write

- **Personal** — auto, home, renters, umbrella, [specialty: boat, motorcycle, valuables].
- **Commercial** — general liability, property, BOP, workers comp, commercial auto, professional liability.
- **Life & health** — term, whole, disability, group benefits.

We are paid by carrier commission of roughly [x]% on new business and [y]% on renewal; disclose fees where required.
kb_templates.playbook:
## Needs analysis

1. What is the expiring premium, and with which carrier? Get the declarations page.
2. When is the X-date? Never let a quote arrive after the renewal has auto-renewed.
3. Losses in the last five years — get the loss runs, not the client's memory.
4. Any lapse, cancellation or non-renewal? It changes which markets will look at the risk.
5. What are they actually worried about? Sell the coverage that answers it.

## Presenting the quote

Show two or three options side by side with the same limits, and name the trade-off in each. Never lead with the cheapest without saying what it drops.
kb_templates.process:
## Lead to bound policy

1. **New Lead** — contact created, X-date and current carrier captured.
2. **Needs Analysis** — dec page, loss runs and exposures collected.
3. **Quoting Carriers** — submissions out to the appointed markets.
4. **Quote Presented** — options compared for the client with limits held constant.
5. **In Underwriting** — carrier questions, inspections and subjectivities being cleared.
6. **Policy Bound** — binder issued, premium paid. Create the Policy record with its renewal date here.
7. **Lost** — record why: price, coverage, declined by underwriting, or stayed with incumbent.
kb_templates.competitors:
## Where we win

Against direct writers ([name them]) we win on choice of carrier and on having a person who answers at claim time. Against [local competitor] we win on [markets, response time, specialty class].

## Where we lose

We lose on rock-bottom price and on classes we have no appointment for. When that happens, note the X-date and re-approach sixty days before renewal.
